div.h1: CLR Record Structure vs UPDATE Record
div.p: Compensation Log Records replace UPDATE records in the undo chain

Idempotency & Recovery: If a crash occurs during an undo operation, recovery reads the CLR (LSN 250), applies the undo data, and follows undo_next_lsn=150. This ensures the system resumes from the correct historical point without attempting to double-undo LSN 200.
UPDATE records store images for both recovery phases:

ul.li: Store undo_data (the before-image of the undone action)
undo_next_lsn ⚡: Pointer to the next record to undo in the transaction's history.

div.p: Crucial: undo_next_lsn is NOT the same as prev_lsn. It skips the record that was just compensated to prevent infinite undo loops.
